{"step_by_step_thinking": "In orthodontics, the placement of retainers is based on principles of force distribution and stability. The fulcrum line is the line along which the forces are applied during tooth movement. To prevent unwanted movement, the indirect retainer is placed as far as possible from the fulcrum line. This is because placing it farther from the fulcrum line increases its effectiveness in resisting movement by creating a greater moment arm, thus providing better stability. The direct retainer is typically placed near the fulcrum line to control movement in the immediate area. The edentulous area is not a standard location for indirect retainers. Therefore, the correct placement of an indirect retainer is as far as possible from the fulcrum line.", "answer_choice": "B"}
